Parent.description:
When set this asset will inherit properties from the named asset.

When inheriting from another SoundEvent most properties will simply be copied from the parent asset to this asset. In the case where both child and parent provide a field the child field will simply replace the value provided by the parent, in the case of nested structures this will apply to the fields within the structure. In some cases the field may decide to act differently, for example: by merging the parent and child fields together.
Parent.markdownDescription:
When set this asset will inherit properties from the named asset.

When inheriting from another **SoundEvent** most properties will simply be copied from the parent asset to this asset. In the case where both child and parent provide a field the child field will simply replace the value provided by the parent, in the case of nested structures this will apply to the fields within the structure. In some cases the field may decide to act differently, for example: by merging the parent and child fields together.

Tags.title: Map
Tags.description:
Tags are a general way to describe an asset that can be interpreted by other systems in a way they see fit.

For example you could tag something with a Material tag with the values Solid and Stone, And another single tag Ore.

Tags will be expanded into a single list of tags automatically. Using the above example with Material and Ore the end result would be the following list of tags: Ore, Material, Solid, Stone, Material=Solid and Material=Stone.
Tags.markdownDescription:
Tags are a general way to describe an asset that can be interpreted by other systems in a way they see fit.

For example you could tag something with a **Material** tag with the values **Solid** and **Stone**, And another single tag **Ore**.

Tags will be expanded into a single list of tags automatically. Using the above example with **Material** and **Ore** the end result would be the following list of tags: **Ore**, **Material**, **Solid**, **Stone**, **Material=Solid** and **Material=Stone**.

Volume.description: Volume adjustment of the sound event in decibels.
Volume.markdownDescription: Volume adjustment of the sound event in decibels.

Pitch.description: Pitch adjustment of the sound event in semitones.
Pitch.markdownDescription: Pitch adjustment of the sound event in semitones.

MusicDuckingVolume.description: Amount to duck music volume when playing in decibels.
MusicDuckingVolume.markdownDescription: Amount to duck music volume when playing in decibels.

AmbientDuckingVolume.description: Amount to duck ambient sounds when playing in decibels.
AmbientDuckingVolume.markdownDescription: Amount to duck ambient sounds when playing in decibels.

StartAttenuationDistance.description: Distance at which to begin attenuation in blocks.
StartAttenuationDistance.markdownDescription: Distance at which to begin attenuation in blocks.

MaxDistance.description: Maximum distance at which this sound event can be heard in blocks (i.e. the distance at which it's attenuated to zero).
MaxDistance.markdownDescription: Maximum distance at which this sound event can be heard in blocks (i.e. the distance at which it's attenuated to zero).

MaxInstance.description: Max concurrent number of instances of this sound event.
MaxInstance.markdownDescription: Max concurrent number of instances of this sound event.

PreventSoundInterruption.description: Whether to prevent interruption of this sound event.
PreventSoundInterruption.markdownDescription: Whether to prevent interruption of this sound event.

Layers.description: The layered sounds that make up this sound event.
Layers.markdownDescription: The layered sounds that make up this sound event.

AudioCategory.description: Audio category to assign this sound event to for additional property routing.
AudioCategory.markdownDescription: Audio category to assign this sound event to for additional property routing.
